mission.title: litellm: [Security]: CRITICAL: Malicious litellm_init.pth in litellm 1.82.8 — credential stealer
mission.description: [Security]: CRITICAL: Malicious litellm_init.pth in litellm 1.82.8 — credential stealer. This issue affects 1108+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
The `litellm==1.82.8` wheel package on PyPI contains a malicious `.pth` file (`litellm_init.pth`, 34,628 bytes) that **automatically executes a credential-stealing script every time the Python interpreter starts** — no `import litellm` required.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Security]: CRITICAL: Malicious litellm_init.pth in litellm…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
litellm_init.pth,sha256=ceNa7wMJnNHy1kRnNCcwJaFjWX3pORLfMh7xGL8TUjg,34628
```

steps.title: Confirm [Security]: CRITICAL: Malicious litellm_init.pth… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/issues/24512
codeSnippets:
pip download litellm==1.82.8 --no-deps -d /tmp/check
python3 -c "
import zipfile, os
whl = '/tmp/check/' + [f for f in os.listdir('/tmp/check') if f.endswith('.whl')][0]
with zipfile.ZipFile(whl) as z:
    pth = [n for n in z.namelist() if n.endswith('.pth')]
    print('PTH files:', pth)
    for p in pth:
        print(z.read(p)[:300])
"

## Malicious Behavior (full analysis)

The payload is **double base64-encoded**. When decoded, it performs the following:

### Stage 1: Information Collection

The script collects sensitive data from the host system:

- **System info**: `hostname`, `whoami`, `uname -a`, `ip addr`, `ip route`
- **Environment variables**: `printenv` (captures all API keys, secrets, tokens)
- **SSH keys**: `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`, `~/.ssh/id_ecdsa`, `~/.ssh/id_dsa`, `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`, `~/.ssh/known_hosts`, `~/.ssh/config`
- **Git credentials**: `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.git-credentials`
- **AWS credentials**: `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`, IMDS token + security credentials
- **Kubernetes secrets**: `~/.kube/config`, `/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf`, `/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf`, `/etc/kube

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
